planMarkdown:
Bot: MemePulse Watcher

Summary:
A Telegram group bot that monitors selected memecoins or tokens, publishes daily trend summaries, and forwards important announcements from official X accounts to subscribed groups.

Product goals:
Provide Telegram communities with concise, scheduled daily insights for selected memecoins or tokens. The bot should track price movement, volume, market-cap changes, sentiment-relevant trend signals, and notable official announcements from X. Group admins can subscribe a group to one or more tokens, configure delivery time, and choose whether announcement alerts are posted immediately or included only in the daily digest. The product should prioritize reliability, clear summaries, and anti-spam controls over speculative trading advice.

High-level architecture:
Run as a single Node.js ESM service using grammY. The process initializes the bot with TELEGRAMBOTTOKEN, connects to MongoDB, loads active subscriptions, and starts polling or webhook handling depending on deployment configuration. Core modules should include: Telegram interface layer for commands and group interactions; subscription manager for group-token configuration; token market data provider abstraction for prices, volume, liquidity, market cap, and historical candles; X announcement provider abstraction for monitoring official project accounts and keywords; insight engine for comparing current metrics against previous snapshots; scheduler for daily digest jobs and periodic announcement checks; notification dispatcher for formatted Telegram messages; and persistence layer using MongoDB. The scheduler runs in-process because the service is a single process. It should maintain job state in MongoDB to avoid duplicate sends after restarts. External APIs should be wrapped behind provider interfaces so data sources can be replaced without changing bot logic. Announcement detection should prefer official X API access where available; if unavailable, use a compliant approved data provider rather than fragile scraping. All outgoing group messages should go through a rate-limited dispatcher.

Data model:
MongoDB collections: users for Telegram user profile, role hints, and first-seen timestamps; groups for Telegram chat ID, title, admin configuration status, timezone, default digest time, language, and enabled flags; tokens for symbol, name, chain, contract address, market-data provider IDs, official website, official X handles, tracking keywords, and verification status; subscriptions for group-to-token mapping, alert preferences, digest schedule, thresholds, enabled metrics, created-by admin, and status; marketsnapshots for token metrics by timestamp including price, 24h change, volume, market cap, liquidity, holder count if available, and source metadata; xposts for tracked announcements with post ID, handle, text, URL, timestamp, classification, and delivered status; digestruns for scheduled job execution state, target group, token, period, generated summary, send status, and deduplication key; auditlogs for admin actions, configuration changes, API errors, and delivery failures. Indexes should cover chat ID, token identity, subscription status, snapshot timestamps, X post IDs, and digest deduplication keys.

Key flows:
Onboarding flow: an admin adds the bot to a group, grants needed permissions, runs /setup, selects a token by symbol or contract address, confirms official X handles, sets timezone and daily digest time, then activates the subscription. Daily insight flow: scheduler finds due subscriptions, fetches latest market data, compares against previous 24h and 7d snapshots, generates a concise trend report, stores a digestrun, and sends the message to the group. Announcement flow: periodic checker fetches new posts from configured official X handles, filters for major announcement signals such as listings, partnerships, launches, burns, audits, governance, tokenomics, exploits, or emergency notices, deduplicates by post ID, then either posts immediately or queues for the next digest based on subscription settings. Manual query flow: users run token commands such as /trend or /price in the group, the bot fetches current data, applies rate limits, and replies with a compact status card. Admin management flow: admins can add or remove tokens, change alert thresholds, pause notifications, and preview the next digest. Failure flow: if external APIs fail, the bot records the failure, retries with backoff, sends degraded summaries using cached data when safe, and avoids posting stale data without labeling it.

Tech stack:
Node.js ESM runtime with grammY for Telegram bot handling. MongoDB for persistence, job state, subscriptions, snapshots, and deduplication. In-process scheduler for daily digests and periodic X checks. Market data provider integrations through HTTP APIs with provider adapters. X announcement integration through official API access or an approved data provider. Environment variables for TELEGRAMBOTTOKEN, MongoDB connection string, market data API credentials, X/API credentials, bot environment, webhook URL if used, and scheduler settings. Logging should be structured JSON. File/image output, if later needed for charts, should use generic object storage or filesystem storage.

Non-functional requirements:
Reliability: persist job state and use deduplication keys so restarts do not create duplicate messages. Scalability: single-process design should batch API calls, cache provider responses, and rate-limit outgoing Telegram messages; if usage grows, optimize within the same service before introducing new infrastructure. Security: only group admins can change subscriptions; store secrets only in environment variables; validate contract addresses and external URLs; avoid accepting arbitrary executable content. Compliance: respect Telegram rules, X access terms, and market data provider rate limits. Accuracy: label all generated insights as informational, not financial advice; show data source timestamps; distinguish confirmed official announcements from keyword-based signals. Observability: log command usage, scheduled sends, provider latency, API failures, and Telegram delivery errors. Maintainability: use clean provider interfaces, separate command handlers from business logic, and keep formatting templates centralized. Anti-spam: enforce per-group command cooldowns, announcement deduplication, alert severity thresholds, and configurable quiet hours.

Bot commands:
• start — Introduces the bot and explains how to configure token insights in a group. (usage: /start) [general]
• setup — Starts the admin setup flow for a group subscription. (usage: /setup) [admin]
• addtoken — Adds a token or memecoin to the current group's watchlist by symbol or contract address. (usage: /addtoken PEPE or /addtoken 0x...) [admin]
• removetoken — Removes a token from the current group's watchlist. (usage: /removetoken PEPE) [admin]
• tokens — Lists all tokens currently tracked in the group. (usage: /tokens) [group]
• trend — Shows the latest trend summary for a tracked token. (usage: /trend PEPE) [insights]
• price — Shows current price, 24h movement, volume, and data timestamp for a token. (usage: /price PEPE) [insights]
• digest — Generates an on-demand daily-style digest for a tracked token. (usage: /digest PEPE) [insights]
• announcements — Shows recent official X announcements detected for a tracked token. (usage: /announcements PEPE) [insights]
• settime — Sets the daily digest delivery time for the group. (usage: /settime 09:00) [admin]
• settimezone — Sets the timezone used for scheduled digests. (usage: /settimezone UTC or /settimezone America/NewYork) [admin]
• alerts — Configures immediate announcement alerts and trend threshold alerts. (usage: /alerts on or /alerts off) [admin]
• pause — Temporarily pauses scheduled digests and alerts for the group. (usage: /pause) [admin]
• resume — Resumes scheduled digests and alerts for the group. (usage: /resume) [admin]
• settings — Displays the group's current bot configuration. (usage: /settings) [admin]
• help — Shows available commands and usage examples. (usage: /help) [general]

envVarDocs.title: Telegram Bot Token
envVarDocs.description: Required token used by grammY to connect this Telegram bot to Telegram transport.
envVarDocs.usage: Set this in the deployment environment. The bot exits with guidance if it is missing.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Open Telegram, search for the verified @BotFather, send /newbot, choose a display name, choose a unique username ending in bot, then copy the token BotFather returns.

envVarDocs.description: Stores groups, tokens, subscriptions, snapshots, X posts, digest runs, audit logs, cursors, and memory.
envVarDocs.usage: If missing, the bot runs with temporary in-memory storage.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Use MongoDB Atlas or another MongoDB provider.

envVarDocs.description: Maximum AI request time in milliseconds.

envVarDocs.howToGet: Set in environment only if needed.

envVarDocs.title: AI Max Retries
envVarDocs.description: Retry count for transient AI gateway failures.
envVarDocs.usage: Defaults to 2.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Set in environment only if needed.

envVarDocs.title: Concurrency
envVarDocs.description: General concurrency control setting reserved for future internal tuning.

envVarDocs.howToGet: Set in environment only if needed.

# Telegram transport. Required. Create this with the verified @BotFather account and keep it secret.

COOKMYBOTS_AI_KEY=
WEB3_CHAT_MODE=on
AI_TIMEOUT_MS=600000
AI_MAX_RETRIES=2
CONCURRENCY=20

# CookMyBots X Proxy for official X announcement monitoring. Optional. Connect X in the CookMyBots UI.
COOKMYBOTS_X_ENDPOINT=
COOKMYBOTS_X_KEY=

# Market data and scheduler controls
COINGECKO_API_BASE=https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3
SCHEDULER_POLL_MS=60000
ANNOUNCEMENT_POLL_MS=300000
DEFAULT_TIMEZONE=UTC
DEFAULT_DIGEST_TIME=09:00

DOCS.md.content:
MemePulse Watcher documentation

What the bot does

MemePulse Watcher helps Telegram groups follow selected memecoins and tokens. It can post daily trend digests, answer manual price and trend commands, and monitor configured official X handles for major announcement signals.

Getting your Telegram bot token

1) Open Telegram.
2) Search for the verified @BotFather account. Use the official verified BotFather account only.
3) Send /newbot to BotFather.
4) Choose a display name for the bot. This is the name Telegram users will see.
5) Choose a unique username for the bot. The username must end in bot, such as MemePulseWatcherBot.
6) Copy the token BotFather returns.
7) Paste the token into the project environment as TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN.
8) Keep the token secret. Do not send it in chat, commit it to source control, or include it in screenshots.

If the token is leaked, use BotFather's token regeneration flow for the bot, update TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN in the deployment environment, and redeploy the bot.

Public commands

/start
Introduces the bot and explains how to configure token insights in a group.
Usage: /start

/help
Shows available commands and usage examples.
Usage: /help

/setup
Initializes the current group for MemePulse tracking. Admin only.
Usage: /setup

/addtoken
Adds a token or memecoin to the current group's watchlist. Admin only.

/reset

Usage: /reset

Environment variables

TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN is required for Telegram transport. Create it with the verified @BotFather account and keep it secret.
MONGODB_URI is optional but recommended. It stores subscriptions, groups, tokens, scheduler dedupe state, X cursors, snapshots, audit logs, and memory.
COOKMYBOTS_AI_ENDPOINT is the CookMyBots AI Gateway base URL.
COOKMYBOTS_AI_KEY enables ChainGPT Web3 summaries through the CookMyBots AI Gateway.
WEB3_CHAT_MODE controls Web3 routing and defaults to on.
COOKMYBOTS_X_ENDPOINT is the CookMyBots X Proxy base URL.
COOKMYBOTS_X_KEY enables X proxy access. If it or COOKMYBOTS_X_ENDPOINT is missing, X announcement features degrade gracefully.
COINGECKO_API_BASE sets the market data provider base URL.
SCHEDULER_POLL_MS controls scheduler polling frequency.
ANNOUNCEMENT_POLL_MS controls X announcement polling frequency.
DEFAULT_TIMEZONE sets the default group timezone.
DEFAULT_DIGEST_TIME sets the default daily digest time.

Setup and run

1) Install dependencies with npm install.
2) Copy .env.sample to .env.
3) Set TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN using the token returned by BotFather.
4) Set MONGODB_URI for persistence.
5) Run npm run dev locally or npm start in production.

Operational notes

Admin commands require Telegram group admin status.
Never ask users to send TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN in chat.
AI features call the CookMyBots AI Gateway with COOKMYBOTS_AI_ENDPOINT and COOKMYBOTS_AI_KEY. The bot does not call OpenAI directly.
X monitoring uses only the CookMyBots X Proxy. The bot does not call X APIs directly.
Daily digests use dedupe keys to avoid duplicate sends when MongoDB is configured.
If MongoDB is missing, the bot still runs with temporary in-memory storage.
All token insights are informational only and are not financial advice.

README.md.content:
MemePulse Watcher Bot

MemePulse Watcher is a Telegram group bot for memecoin and token communities. It tracks selected tokens, posts daily market digests, answers manual price and trend requests, and can monitor configured official X handles for major announcement signals.

Features

1) Group admin setup with /setup.
2) Token watchlists per Telegram group.
3) Live price cards from CoinGecko.
4) Daily-style trend and digest summaries.
5) Optional ChainGPT-powered Web3 digest wording through the CookMyBots AI Gateway.
6) Optional official X announcement detection through the CookMyBots X Proxy.
7) MongoDB persistence for subscriptions, snapshots, dedupe keys, X posts, audit logs, and memory.
8) In-memory fallback when MONGODB_URI is not configured.
9) Anti-spam command cooldowns and rate-limited Telegram sends.
10) Admin-only configuration commands.

Architecture

The bot runs as one Node.js ESM process using grammY and @grammyjs/runner long polling. Commands live in src/commands. Provider adapters live in src/services. Shared configuration, logging, MongoDB, memory, admin checks, and rate limiting live in src/lib.

The scheduler runs in-process. It polls due subscriptions, stores digest run dedupe keys, and avoids duplicate daily posts after restarts when MongoDB is configured.

Getting your Telegram bot token

1) Open Telegram.
2) Search for the verified @BotFather account. Make sure it is the official verified BotFather account before sending any setup commands.
3) Send /newbot to BotFather.
4) Choose a display name for the bot. This is the friendly name users see in Telegram.
5) Choose a unique username for the bot. Telegram requires it to end in bot, such as MemePulseWatcherBot.
6) BotFather will return a token that looks like a long string with numbers, a colon, and letters.
7) Copy that token and paste it into this project environment as TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN.
8) Keep the token secret. Do not paste it into Telegram chats, documentation, screenshots, source code, or support messages.

If the token is leaked, open BotFather again and use its token regeneration flow for your bot. After regenerating the token, update TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN in your deployment environment and redeploy the service.

Setup

1) Install dependencies with npm install.
2) Copy .env.sample to .env.
3) Set TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN using the token from BotFather.
4) Set MONGODB_URI for persistent production use.
5) Optional: set COOKMYBOTS_AI_KEY for ChainGPT summaries.
6) Optional: set COOKMYBOTS_X_ENDPOINT and COOKMYBOTS_X_KEY, then connect X in CookMyBots UI.

Commands

/start welcomes users and explains group setup.
/help lists all commands and points maintainers to the docs for BotFather token setup.
/setup initializes the current group. Admin only.
/addtoken PEPE adds a token. Admin only.
/addtoken PEPE x=officialHandle adds a token and official X handle. Admin only.
/removetoken PEPE removes a token. Admin only.
/tokens lists group tokens.
/price PEPE shows current price, 24h move, volume, market cap, and timestamp.
/trend PEPE shows a compact trend summary.
/digest PEPE generates an on-demand daily-style digest.
/announcements PEPE shows recent detected official X posts.
/settime 09:00 sets the daily digest time. Admin only.
/settimezone UTC sets the digest timezone. Admin only.
/alerts on enables immediate major announcement alerts. Admin only.
/alerts off disables immediate major announcement alerts. Admin only.
/pause pauses scheduled digests and alerts. Admin only.
/resume resumes scheduled digests and alerts. Admin only.
/settings shows group configuration. Admin only.
/reset clears the user's memory for the current chat.

Integrations

CoinGecko is used for public market data through COINGECKO_API_BASE. The bot uses search, contract lookup, and simple price endpoints. Provider failures are logged and user-facing errors are kept short.

CookMyBots AI Gateway is used for Web3 digest wording via /chaingpt/chat. The bot never calls OpenAI or ChainGPT directly and never stores AI keys outside the deployment environment. Requests use COOKMYBOTS_AI_ENDPOINT with Authorization: Bearer COOKMYBOTS_AI_KEY.

CookMyBots X Proxy is used for X data. The bot never calls api.x.com or api.twitter.com directly and never handles OAuth tokens. Users must connect an X account in the CookMyBots UI. If COOKMYBOTS_X_ENDPOINT or COOKMYBOTS_X_KEY is missing, /announcements and alert monitoring degrade gracefully instead of crashing.

Database

MongoDB collections used by the bot:

1) users for Telegram user profile metadata.
2) groups for chat configuration, timezone, digest time, and enabled state.
3) tokens for symbol, provider IDs, official X handles, and keywords.
4) subscriptions for group-token mapping and alert preferences.
5) marketsnapshots for token metrics over time.
6) xposts for detected official posts and delivery status.
7) digestruns for scheduled digest dedupe and status.
8) auditlogs for admin actions and failures.
9) memory_messages for long-term user memory.
10) x_cursors for persistent X polling checkpoints.

Indexes are created only on application fields. The bot does not create indexes on _id. Update and upsert operations keep createdAt insert-only and write updatedAt separately.

Run locally

npm run dev

Build

npm run build

Start

npm start

Deployment

Deploy as one Render service or equivalent Node.js service. The start command is npm start. The build command is npm run build. Required env var is TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN. MONGODB_URI is strongly recommended for production persistence.

On boot, the bot logs environment sanity as booleans only. It does not print Telegram tokens, MongoDB credentials, CookMyBots keys, or Authorization headers.

Troubleshooting

If the bot exits immediately, check TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN is set and was copied exactly from BotFather.
If the token was leaked, regenerate it in BotFather, update TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN in the deployment environment, and redeploy.
If subscriptions vanish after restart, set MONGODB_URI.
If X announcements do not work, set COOKMYBOTS_X_ENDPOINT and COOKMYBOTS_X_KEY and connect X in CookMyBots.
If AI summaries are unavailable, set COOKMYBOTS_AI_KEY.
Use /settings in a group to verify digest time, timezone, enabled state, and tracked tokens.

Notes

All market insights are informational only and are not financial advice.

help.js.content:
export default function register(bot) {
  bot.command("help", async (ctx) => {
    await ctx.reply(
      [
        "MemePulse Watcher commands",
        "/start - intro and setup guidance",
        "/setup - initialize this group",
        "/addtoken PEPE or /addtoken PEPE x=handle - add a token",
        "/removetoken PEPE - remove a token",
        "/tokens - list tracked tokens",
        "/price PEPE - current price card",
        "/trend PEPE - latest trend summary",
        "/digest PEPE - on-demand daily digest",
        "/announcements PEPE - recent detected official X posts",
        "/settime 09:00 - set daily digest time",
        "/settimezone UTC - set timezone",
        "/alerts on or /alerts off - toggle immediate major X alerts",
        "/pause - pause scheduled posts",
        "/resume - resume scheduled posts",
        "/settings - show group configuration",
        "/reset - clear your bot memory",
        "To run the bot, create a Telegram token with the verified @BotFather and set it as TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN in the project environment. Never send tokens in chat; see README.md or DOCS.md for the setup steps.",
        "Admin commands require group admin permissions. All insights are informational, not financial advice."
      ].join("\n")
    );
  });
}

state.planMarkdown:
Bot: MemePulse Watcher

Summary:
A Telegram group bot that monitors selected memecoins or tokens, publishes daily trend summaries, and forwards important announcements from official X accounts to subscribed groups.

Product goals:
Provide Telegram communities with concise, scheduled daily insights for selected memecoins or tokens. The bot should track price movement, volume, market-cap changes, sentiment-relevant trend signals, and notable official announcements from X. Group admins can subscribe a group to one or more tokens, configure delivery time, and choose whether announcement alerts are posted immediately or included only in the daily digest. The product should prioritize reliability, clear summaries, and anti-spam controls over speculative trading advice.

High-level architecture:
Run as a single Node.js ESM service using grammY. The process initializes the bot with TELEGRAMBOTTOKEN, connects to MongoDB, loads active subscriptions, and starts polling or webhook handling depending on deployment configuration. Core modules should include: Telegram interface layer for commands and group interactions; subscription manager for group-token configuration; token market data provider abstraction for prices, volume, liquidity, market cap, and historical candles; X announcement provider abstraction for monitoring official project accounts and keywords; insight engine for comparing current metrics against previous snapshots; scheduler for daily digest jobs and periodic announcement checks; notification dispatcher for formatted Telegram messages; and persistence layer using MongoDB. The scheduler runs in-process because the service is a single process. It should maintain job state in MongoDB to avoid duplicate sends after restarts. External APIs should be wrapped behind provider interfaces so data sources can be replaced without changing bot logic. Announcement detection should prefer official X API access where available; if unavailable, use a compliant approved data provider rather than fragile scraping. All outgoing group messages should go through a rate-limited dispatcher.

Data model:
MongoDB collections: users for Telegram user profile, role hints, and first-seen timestamps; groups for Telegram chat ID, title, admin configuration status, timezone, default digest time, language, and enabled flags; tokens for symbol, name, chain, contract address, market-data provider IDs, official website, official X handles, tracking keywords, and verification status; subscriptions for group-to-token mapping, alert preferences, digest schedule, thresholds, enabled metrics, created-by admin, and status; marketsnapshots for token metrics by timestamp including price, 24h change, volume, market cap, liquidity, holder count if available, and source metadata; xposts for tracked announcements with post ID, handle, text, URL, timestamp, classification, and delivered status; digestruns for scheduled job execution state, target group, token, period, generated summary, send status, and deduplication key; auditlogs for admin actions, configuration changes, API errors, and delivery failures. Indexes should cover chat ID, token identity, subscription status, snapshot timestamps, X post IDs, and digest deduplication keys.

Key flows:
Onboarding flow: an admin adds the bot to a group, grants needed permissions, runs /setup, selects a token by symbol or contract address, confirms official X handles, sets timezone and daily digest time, then activates the subscription. Daily insight flow: scheduler finds due subscriptions, fetches latest market data, compares against previous 24h and 7d snapshots, generates a concise trend report, stores a digestrun, and sends the message to the group. Announcement flow: periodic checker fetches new posts from configured official X handles, filters for major announcement signals such as listings, partnerships, launches, burns, audits, governance, tokenomics, exploits, or emergency notices, deduplicates by post ID, then either posts immediately or queues for the next digest based on subscription settings. Manual query flow: users run token commands such as /trend or /price in the group, the bot fetches current data, applies rate limits, and replies with a compact status card. Admin management flow: admins can add or remove tokens, change alert thresholds, pause notifications, and preview the next digest. Failure flow: if external APIs fail, the bot records the failure, retries with backoff, sends degraded summaries using cached data when safe, and avoids posting stale data without labeling it.

Tech stack:
Node.js ESM runtime with grammY for Telegram bot handling. MongoDB for persistence, job state, subscriptions, snapshots, and deduplication. In-process scheduler for daily digests and periodic X checks. Market data provider integrations through HTTP APIs with provider adapters. X announcement integration through official API access or an approved data provider. Environment variables for TELEGRAMBOTTOKEN, MongoDB connection string, market data API credentials, X/API credentials, bot environment, webhook URL if used, and scheduler settings. Logging should be structured JSON. File/image output, if later needed for charts, should use generic object storage or filesystem storage.

Non-functional requirements:
Reliability: persist job state and use deduplication keys so restarts do not create duplicate messages. Scalability: single-process design should batch API calls, cache provider responses, and rate-limit outgoing Telegram messages; if usage grows, optimize within the same service before introducing new infrastructure. Security: only group admins can change subscriptions; store secrets only in environment variables; validate contract addresses and external URLs; avoid accepting arbitrary executable content. Compliance: respect Telegram rules, X access terms, and market data provider rate limits. Accuracy: label all generated insights as informational, not financial advice; show data source timestamps; distinguish confirmed official announcements from keyword-based signals. Observability: log command usage, scheduled sends, provider latency, API failures, and Telegram delivery errors. Maintainability: use clean provider interfaces, separate command handlers from business logic, and keep formatting templates centralized. Anti-spam: enforce per-group command cooldowns, announcement deduplication, alert severity thresholds, and configurable quiet hours.

Bot commands:
• start — Introduces the bot and explains how to configure token insights in a group. (usage: /start) [general]
• setup — Starts the admin setup flow for a group subscription. (usage: /setup) [admin]
• addtoken — Adds a token or memecoin to the current group's watchlist by symbol or contract address. (usage: /addtoken PEPE or /addtoken 0x...) [admin]
• removetoken — Removes a token from the current group's watchlist. (usage: /removetoken PEPE) [admin]
• tokens — Lists all tokens currently tracked in the group. (usage: /tokens) [group]
• trend — Shows the latest trend summary for a tracked token. (usage: /trend PEPE) [insights]
• price — Shows current price, 24h movement, volume, and data timestamp for a token. (usage: /price PEPE) [insights]
• digest — Generates an on-demand daily-style digest for a tracked token. (usage: /digest PEPE) [insights]
• announcements — Shows recent official X announcements detected for a tracked token. (usage: /announcements PEPE) [insights]
• settime — Sets the daily digest delivery time for the group. (usage: /settime 09:00) [admin]
• settimezone — Sets the timezone used for scheduled digests. (usage: /settimezone UTC or /settimezone America/NewYork) [admin]
• alerts — Configures immediate announcement alerts and trend threshold alerts. (usage: /alerts on or /alerts off) [admin]
• pause — Temporarily pauses scheduled digests and alerts for the group. (usage: /pause) [admin]
• resume — Resumes scheduled digests and alerts for the group. (usage: /resume) [admin]
• settings — Displays the group's current bot configuration. (usage: /settings) [admin]
• help — Shows available commands and usage examples. (usage: /help) [general]

planSections.productGoals: Provide Telegram communities with concise, scheduled daily insights for selected memecoins or tokens. The bot should track price movement, volume, market-cap changes, sentiment-relevant trend signals, and notable official announcements from X. The product should prioritize reliability, clear summaries, and anti-spam controls over speculative trading advice.

DOCS.md.content:
MemePulse Watcher documentation

What the bot does

MemePulse Watcher helps Telegram groups follow selected memecoins and tokens. It can post daily trend digests, answer manual price and trend commands, and monitor configured official X handles for major announcement signals.

Getting your Telegram bot token

1) Open Telegram.
2) Search for the verified @BotFather account. Use the official verified BotFather account only.
3) Send /newbot to BotFather.
4) Choose a display name for the bot. This is the name Telegram users will see.
5) Choose a unique username for the bot. The username must end in bot, such as MemePulseWatcherBot.
6) Copy the token BotFather returns.
7) Paste the token into the project environment as TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN.
8) Keep the token secret. Do not send it in chat, commit it to source control, or include it in screenshots.

If the token is leaked, use BotFather's token regeneration flow for the bot, update TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN in the deployment environment, and redeploy the bot.

Public commands

/start
Introduces the bot and explains how to configure token insights in a group.
Usage: /start

/help
Shows available commands and usage examples.
Usage: /help

/setup
Initializes the current group for MemePulse tracking. Admin only.
Usage: /setup

/addtoken
Adds a token or memecoin to the current group's watchlist. Admin only.
Usage: /addtoken PEPE
Usage with X handle: /addtoken PEPE x=pepecoineth

/removetoken
Removes a token from the current group's watchlist. Admin only.
Usage: /removetoken PEPE

/tokens
Lists all tokens currently tracked in the group.
Usage: /tokens

/price
Shows current price, 24h movement, volume, market cap, and data timestamp.
Usage: /price PEPE

/trend
Shows a compact trend summary for a tracked token.
Usage: /trend PEPE

/digest
Generates an on-demand daily-style digest for a token.
Usage: /digest PEPE

/announcements
Shows recent official X announcements detected for a token.
Usage: /announcements PEPE

/settime
Sets the daily digest delivery time for the group. Admin only.
Usage: /settime 09:00

/settimezone
Sets the timezone used for scheduled digests. Admin only.
Usage: /settimezone UTC
Usage: /settimezone America/New_York

/alerts
Configures immediate announcement alerts. Admin only.
Usage: /alerts on
Usage: /alerts off

/pause
Temporarily pauses scheduled digests and alerts for the group. Admin only.
Usage: /pause

/resume
Resumes scheduled digests and alerts for the group. Admin only.
Usage: /resume

/settings
Displays the group's current bot configuration. Admin only.
Usage: /settings

/reset
Clears the user's saved bot memory for the current chat.
Usage: /reset

Environment variables

TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN is required for Telegram transport. Create it with the verified @BotFather account and keep it secret.
MONGODB_URI is optional but recommended. It stores subscriptions, groups, tokens, scheduler dedupe state, X cursors, snapshots, audit logs, and memory.
COOKMYBOTS_AI_ENDPOINT is the CookMyBots AI Gateway base URL.
COOKMYBOTS_AI_KEY enables ChainGPT Web3 summaries through the CookMyBots AI Gateway.
WEB3_CHAT_MODE controls Web3 routing and defaults to on.
COOKMYBOTS_X_ENDPOINT is the CookMyBots X Proxy base URL.
COOKMYBOTS_X_KEY enables X proxy access. If it or COOKMYBOTS_X_ENDPOINT is missing, X announcement features degrade gracefully.
COINGECKO_API_BASE sets the market data provider base URL.
SCHEDULER_POLL_MS controls scheduler polling frequency.
ANNOUNCEMENT_POLL_MS controls X announcement polling frequency.
DEFAULT_TIMEZONE sets the default group timezone.
DEFAULT_DIGEST_TIME sets the default daily digest time.

Setup and run

1) Install dependencies with npm install.
2) Copy .env.sample to .env.
3) Set TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN using the token returned by BotFather.
4) Set MONGODB_URI for persistence.
5) Run npm run dev locally or npm start in production.

Operational notes

Admin commands require Telegram group admin status.
Never ask users to send TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN in chat.
AI features call the CookMyBots AI Gateway with COOKMYBOTS_AI_ENDPOINT and COOKMYBOTS_AI_KEY. The bot does not call OpenAI directly.
X monitoring uses only the CookMyBots X Proxy. The bot does not call X APIs directly.
Daily digests use dedupe keys to avoid duplicate sends when MongoDB is configured.
If MongoDB is missing, the bot still runs with temporary in-memory storage.
All token insights are informational only and are not financial advice.

README.md.content:
MemePulse Watcher Bot

MemePulse Watcher is a Telegram group bot for memecoin and token communities. It tracks selected tokens, posts daily market digests, answers manual price and trend requests, and can monitor configured official X handles for major announcement signals.

Features

1) Group admin setup with /setup.
2) Token watchlists per Telegram group.
3) Live price cards from CoinGecko.
4) Daily-style trend and digest summaries.
5) Optional ChainGPT-powered Web3 digest wording through the CookMyBots AI Gateway.
6) Optional official X announcement detection through the CookMyBots X Proxy.
7) MongoDB persistence for subscriptions, snapshots, dedupe keys, X posts, audit logs, and memory.
8) In-memory fallback when MONGODB_URI is not configured.
9) Anti-spam command cooldowns and rate-limited Telegram sends.
10) Admin-only configuration commands.

Architecture

The bot runs as one Node.js ESM process using grammY and @grammyjs/runner long polling. Commands live in src/commands. Provider adapters live in src/services. Shared configuration, logging, MongoDB, memory, admin checks, and rate limiting live in src/lib.

The scheduler runs in-process. It polls due subscriptions, stores digest run dedupe keys, and avoids duplicate daily posts after restarts when MongoDB is configured.

Getting your Telegram bot token

1) Open Telegram.
2) Search for the verified @BotFather account. Make sure it is the official verified BotFather account before sending any setup commands.
3) Send /newbot to BotFather.
4) Choose a display name for the bot. This is the friendly name users see in Telegram.
5) Choose a unique username for the bot. Telegram requires it to end in bot, such as MemePulseWatcherBot.
6) BotFather will return a token that looks like a long string with numbers, a colon, and letters.
7) Copy that token and paste it into this project environment as TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN.
8) Keep the token secret. Do not paste it into Telegram chats, documentation, screenshots, source code, or support messages.

If the token is leaked, open BotFather again and use its token regeneration flow for your bot. After regenerating the token, update TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN in your deployment environment and redeploy the service.

Setup

1) Install dependencies with npm install.
2) Copy .env.sample to .env.
3) Set TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN using the token from BotFather.
4) Set MONGODB_URI for persistent production use.
5) Optional: set COOKMYBOTS_AI_KEY for ChainGPT summaries.
6) Optional: set COOKMYBOTS_X_ENDPOINT and COOKMYBOTS_X_KEY, then connect X in CookMyBots UI.

Commands

/start welcomes users and explains group setup.
/help lists all commands and points maintainers to the docs for BotFather token setup.
/setup initializes the current group. Admin only.
/addtoken PEPE adds a token. Admin only.
/addtoken PEPE x=officialHandle adds a token and official X handle. Admin only.
/removetoken PEPE removes a token. Admin only.
/tokens lists group tokens.
/price PEPE shows current price, 24h move, volume, market cap, and timestamp.
/trend PEPE shows a compact trend summary.
/digest PEPE generates an on-demand daily-style digest.
/announcements PEPE shows recent detected official X posts.
/settime 09:00 sets the daily digest time. Admin only.
/settimezone UTC sets the digest timezone. Admin only.
/alerts on enables immediate major announcement alerts. Admin only.
/alerts off disables immediate major announcement alerts. Admin only.
/pause pauses scheduled digests and alerts. Admin only.
/resume resumes scheduled digests and alerts. Admin only.
/settings shows group configuration. Admin only.
/reset clears the user's memory for the current chat.

Integrations

CoinGecko is used for public market data through COINGECKO_API_BASE. The bot uses search, contract lookup, and simple price endpoints. Provider failures are logged and user-facing errors are kept short.

CookMyBots AI Gateway is used for Web3 digest wording via /chaingpt/chat. The bot never calls OpenAI or ChainGPT directly and never stores AI keys outside the deployment environment. Requests use COOKMYBOTS_AI_ENDPOINT with Authorization: Bearer COOKMYBOTS_AI_KEY.

CookMyBots X Proxy is used for X data. The bot never calls api.x.com or api.twitter.com directly and never handles OAuth tokens. Users must connect an X account in the CookMyBots UI. If COOKMYBOTS_X_ENDPOINT or COOKMYBOTS_X_KEY is missing, /announcements and alert monitoring degrade gracefully instead of crashing.

Database

MongoDB collections used by the bot:

1) users for Telegram user profile metadata.
2) groups for chat configuration, timezone, digest time, and enabled state.
3) tokens for symbol, provider IDs, official X handles, and keywords.
4) subscriptions for group-token mapping and alert preferences.
5) marketsnapshots for token metrics over time.
6) xposts for detected official posts and delivery status.
7) digestruns for scheduled digest dedupe and status.
8) auditlogs for admin actions and failures.
9) memory_messages for long-term user memory.
10) x_cursors for persistent X polling checkpoints.

Indexes are created only on application fields. The bot does not create indexes on _id. Update and upsert operations keep createdAt insert-only and write updatedAt separately.

Run locally

npm run dev

Build

npm run build

Start

npm start

Deployment

Deploy as one Render service or equivalent Node.js service. The start command is npm start. The build command is npm run build. Required env var is TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN. MONGODB_URI is strongly recommended for production persistence.

On boot, the bot logs environment sanity as booleans only. It does not print Telegram tokens, MongoDB credentials, CookMyBots keys, or Authorization headers.

Troubleshooting

If the bot exits immediately, check TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN is set and was copied exactly from BotFather.
If the token was leaked, regenerate it in BotFather, update TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN in the deployment environment, and redeploy.
If subscriptions vanish after restart, set MONGODB_URI.
If X announcements do not work, set COOKMYBOTS_X_ENDPOINT and COOKMYBOTS_X_KEY and connect X in CookMyBots.
If AI summaries are unavailable, set COOKMYBOTS_AI_KEY.
Use /settings in a group to verify digest time, timezone, enabled state, and tracked tokens.

Notes

All market insights are informational only and are not financial advice.

help.js.content:
export default function register(bot) {
  bot.command("help", async (ctx) => {
    await ctx.reply(
      [
        "MemePulse Watcher commands",
        "/start - intro and setup guidance",
        "/setup - initialize this group",
        "/addtoken PEPE or /addtoken PEPE x=handle - add a token",
        "/removetoken PEPE - remove a token",
        "/tokens - list tracked tokens",
        "/price PEPE - current price card",
        "/trend PEPE - latest trend summary",
        "/digest PEPE - on-demand daily digest",
        "/announcements PEPE - recent detected official X posts",
        "/settime 09:00 - set daily digest time",
        "/settimezone UTC - set timezone",
        "/alerts on or /alerts off - toggle immediate major X alerts",
        "/pause - pause scheduled posts",
        "/resume - resume scheduled posts",
        "/settings - show group configuration",
        "/reset - clear your bot memory",
        "To run the bot, create a Telegram token with the verified @BotFather and set it as TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN in the project environment. Never send tokens in chat; see README.md or DOCS.md for the setup steps.",
        "Admin commands require group admin permissions. All insights are informational, not financial advice."
      ].join("\n")
    );
  });
}

export const cfg = {
  TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN: process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN || "",
  MONGODB_URI: process.env.MONGODB_URI || "",

  COOKMYBOTS_AI_ENDPOINT: (process.env.COOKMYBOTS_AI_ENDPOINT || "https://api.cookmybots.com/api/ai").replace(/\/+$/, ""),
  COOKMYBOTS_AI_KEY: process.env.COOKMYBOTS_AI_KEY || "",
  WEB3_CHAT_MODE: process.env.WEB3_CHAT_MODE || "on",
  AI_TIMEOUT_MS: Number(process.env.AI_TIMEOUT_MS || 600000),
  AI_MAX_RETRIES: Number(process.env.AI_MAX_RETRIES || 2),
  CONCURRENCY: Number(process.env.CONCURRENCY || 20),

  COOKMYBOTS_X_ENDPOINT: (process.env.COOKMYBOTS_X_ENDPOINT || "").replace(/\/+$/, ""),
  COOKMYBOTS_X_KEY: process.env.COOKMYBOTS_X_KEY || "",

  COINGECKO_API_BASE: (process.env.COINGECKO_API_BASE || "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3").replace(/\/+$/, ""),
  SCHEDULER_POLL_MS: Number(process.env.SCHEDULER_POLL_MS || 60000),
  ANNOUNCEMENT_POLL_MS: Number(process.env.ANNOUNCEMENT_POLL_MS || 300000),
  DEFAULT_TIMEZONE: process.env.DEFAULT_TIMEZONE || "UTC",
  DEFAULT_DIGEST_TIME: process.env.DEFAULT_DIGEST_TIME || "09:00",
  NODE_ENV: process.env.NODE_ENV || "development",
};

export function envHealth() {
  return {
    telegramTokenSet: Boolean(cfg.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN),
    mongoSet: Boolean(cfg.MONGODB_URI),
    aiEndpointSet: Boolean(cfg.COOKMYBOTS_AI_ENDPOINT),
    aiKeySet: Boolean(cfg.COOKMYBOTS_AI_KEY),
    xEndpointSet: Boolean(cfg.COOKMYBOTS_X_ENDPOINT),
    xKeySet: Boolean(cfg.COOKMYBOTS_X_KEY),
  };
}

envVarDocs.title: Telegram Bot Token
envVarDocs.description: Required token used by grammY to connect this Telegram bot to Telegram transport.
envVarDocs.usage: Set this in the deployment environment. The bot exits with guidance if it is missing.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Open Telegram, search for the verified @BotFather, send /newbot, choose a display name, choose a unique username ending in bot, then copy the token BotFather returns.

envVarDocs.title: MongoDB URI
envVarDocs.description: Stores groups, tokens, subscriptions, snapshots, X posts, digest runs, audit logs, cursors, and memory.
envVarDocs.usage: If missing, the bot runs with temporary in-memory storage.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Use MongoDB Atlas or another MongoDB provider.

envVarDocs.title: CookMyBots AI Endpoint
envVarDocs.description: Base URL for CookMyBots AI Gateway.
envVarDocs.usage: Used for ChainGPT Web3 digest summaries. The bot appends AI routes internally.
envVarDocs.howToGet: CookMyBots provides this endpoint.

envVarDocs.title: CookMyBots AI Key
envVarDocs.description: Secret key for CookMyBots AI Gateway.
envVarDocs.usage: Enables AI-generated Web3 summaries. If missing, deterministic summaries are used.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Provided by CookMyBots.

envVarDocs.title: Web3 Chat Mode
envVarDocs.description: Controls ChainGPT routing behavior.
envVarDocs.usage: Defaults to on for this crypto bot.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Set in environment if you need to override behavior.

envVarDocs.title: AI Timeout
envVarDocs.description: Maximum AI request time in milliseconds.
envVarDocs.usage: Defaults to 600000 for long media or reasoning calls.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Set in environment only if needed.

envVarDocs.title: AI Max Retries
envVarDocs.description: Retry count for transient AI gateway failures.
envVarDocs.usage: Defaults to 2.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Set in environment only if needed.

envVarDocs.title: Concurrency
envVarDocs.description: General concurrency control setting reserved for future internal tuning.

envVarDocs.howToGet: Set in environment only if needed.

state.planMarkdown:
Bot: MemePulse Watcher

Summary:
A Telegram group bot that monitors selected memecoins or tokens, publishes daily trend summaries, and forwards important announcements from official X accounts to subscribed groups.

Product goals:
Provide Telegram communities with concise, scheduled daily insights for selected memecoins or tokens. The bot should track price movement, volume, market-cap changes, sentiment-relevant trend signals, and notable official announcements from X. Group admins can subscribe a group to one or more tokens, configure delivery time, and choose whether announcement alerts are posted immediately or included only in the daily digest. The product should prioritize reliability, clear summaries, and anti-spam controls over speculative trading advice.

High-level architecture:
Run as a single Node.js ESM service using grammY. The process initializes the bot with TELEGRAMBOTTOKEN, connects to MongoDB, loads active subscriptions, and starts polling or webhook handling depending on deployment configuration. Core modules should include: Telegram interface layer for commands and group interactions; subscription manager for group-token configuration; token market data provider abstraction for prices, volume, liquidity, market cap, and historical candles; X announcement provider abstraction for monitoring official project accounts and keywords; insight engine for comparing current metrics against previous snapshots; scheduler for daily digest jobs and periodic announcement checks; notification dispatcher for formatted Telegram messages; and persistence layer using MongoDB. The scheduler runs in-process because the service is a single process. It should maintain job state in MongoDB to avoid duplicate sends after restarts. External APIs should be wrapped behind provider interfaces so data sources can be replaced without changing bot logic. Announcement detection should prefer official X API access where available; if unavailable, use a compliant approved data provider rather than fragile scraping. All outgoing group messages should go through a rate-limited dispatcher.

Data model:
MongoDB collections: users for Telegram user profile, role hints, and first-seen timestamps; groups for Telegram chat ID, title, admin configuration status, timezone, default digest time, language, and enabled flags; tokens for symbol, name, chain, contract address, market-data provider IDs, official website, official X handles, tracking keywords, and verification status; subscriptions for group-to-token mapping, alert preferences, digest schedule, thresholds, enabled metrics, created-by admin, and status; marketsnapshots for token metrics by timestamp including price, 24h change, volume, market cap, liquidity, holder count if available, and source metadata; xposts for tracked announcements with post ID, handle, text, URL, timestamp, classification, and delivered status; digestruns for scheduled job execution state, target group, token, period, generated summary, send status, and deduplication key; auditlogs for admin actions, configuration changes, API errors, and delivery failures. Indexes should cover chat ID, token identity, subscription status, snapshot timestamps, X post IDs, and digest deduplication keys.

Key flows:
Onboarding flow: an admin adds the bot to a group, grants needed permissions, runs /setup, selects a token by symbol or contract address, confirms official X handles, sets timezone and daily digest time, then activates the subscription. Daily insight flow: scheduler finds due subscriptions, fetches latest market data, compares against previous 24h and 7d snapshots, generates a concise trend report, stores a digestrun, and sends the message to the group. Announcement flow: periodic checker fetches new posts from configured official X handles, filters for major announcement signals such as listings, partnerships, launches, burns, audits, governance, tokenomics, exploits, or emergency notices, deduplicates by post ID, then either posts immediately or queues for the next digest based on subscription settings. Manual query flow: users run token commands such as /trend or /price in the group, the bot fetches current data, applies rate limits, and replies with a compact status card. Admin management flow: admins can add or remove tokens, change alert thresholds, pause notifications, and preview the next digest. Failure flow: if external APIs fail, the bot records the failure, retries with backoff, sends degraded summaries using cached data when safe, and avoids posting stale data without labeling it.

Tech stack:
Node.js ESM runtime with grammY for Telegram bot handling. MongoDB for persistence, job state, subscriptions, snapshots, and deduplication. In-process scheduler for daily digests and periodic X checks. Market data provider integrations through HTTP APIs with provider adapters. X announcement integration through official API access or an approved data provider. Environment variables for TELEGRAMBOTTOKEN, MongoDB connection string, market data API credentials, X/API credentials, bot environment, webhook URL if used, and scheduler settings. Logging should be structured JSON. File/image output, if later needed for charts, should use generic object storage or filesystem storage.

Non-functional requirements:
Reliability: persist job state and use deduplication keys so restarts do not create duplicate messages. Scalability: single-process design should batch API calls, cache provider responses, and rate-limit outgoing Telegram messages; if usage grows, optimize within the same service before introducing new infrastructure. Security: only group admins can change subscriptions; store secrets only in environment variables; validate contract addresses and external URLs; avoid accepting arbitrary executable content. Compliance: respect Telegram rules, X access terms, and market data provider rate limits. Accuracy: label all generated insights as informational, not financial advice; show data source timestamps; distinguish confirmed official announcements from keyword-based signals. Observability: log command usage, scheduled sends, provider latency, API failures, and Telegram delivery errors. Maintainability: use clean provider interfaces, separate command handlers from business logic, and keep formatting templates centralized. Anti-spam: enforce per-group command cooldowns, announcement deduplication, alert severity thresholds, and configurable quiet hours.

Bot commands:
• start — Introduces the bot and explains how to configure token insights in a group. (usage: /start) [general]
• setup — Starts the admin setup flow for a group subscription. (usage: /setup) [admin]
• addtoken — Adds a token or memecoin to the current group's watchlist by symbol or contract address. (usage: /addtoken PEPE or /addtoken 0x...) [admin]
• removetoken — Removes a token from the current group's watchlist. (usage: /removetoken PEPE) [admin]
• tokens — Lists all tokens currently tracked in the group. (usage: /tokens) [group]
• trend — Shows the latest trend summary for a tracked token. (usage: /trend PEPE) [insights]
• price — Shows current price, 24h movement, volume, and data timestamp for a token. (usage: /price PEPE) [insights]
• digest — Generates an on-demand daily-style digest for a tracked token. (usage: /digest PEPE) [insights]
• announcements — Shows recent official X announcements detected for a tracked token. (usage: /announcements PEPE) [insights]
• settime — Sets the daily digest delivery time for the group. (usage: /settime 09:00) [admin]
• settimezone — Sets the timezone used for scheduled digests. (usage: /settimezone UTC or /settimezone America/NewYork) [admin]
• alerts — Configures immediate announcement alerts and trend threshold alerts. (usage: /alerts on or /alerts off) [admin]
• pause — Temporarily pauses scheduled digests and alerts for the group. (usage: /pause) [admin]
• resume — Resumes scheduled digests and alerts for the group. (usage: /resume) [admin]
• settings — Displays the group's current bot configuration. (usage: /settings) [admin]
• help — Shows available commands and usage examples. (usage: /help) [general]
